source: TraceCoder — Trace-Driven Multi-Agent Debugging with HLLM
summary: ICSE 2026 paper introducing a Historical Lesson Learning Mechanism (HLLM) that distills insights from prior failed repair attempts into a retrievable lesson store, achieving 34.43% relative improvement in Pass@1 accuracy over baselines.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly maps to limitation #8 (no cross-run agent memory); HLLM pattern is implementable as a Prisma table + prompt injection at the Planning stage

summary: June 2026 paper from Northwestern/Stanford/Cornell/All Hands AI showing frontier LLM agents waste 28–64% of tokens on doomed trajectories; a rolling-cost estimator with early-stop achieved 44.6% average token savings.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly addresses limitation #6 (no test-time compute scaling) and #5 (no graceful degradation); budget-exceeded early abort maps to AIBlocked state

source: Anthropic Effective Harnesses Guide + Agent2Agent SSE Streaming Patterns
summary: Anthropic engineering post recommending structured progress events from long-running agents for user trust and early course-correction; complements the 2026 A2A protocol's real-time partial artifact streaming pattern.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — maps to limitation #9 (no streaming/incremental output); AgentForge already has Fastify SSE and React dashboard infrastructure

summary: March 2026 paper showing that speculative pre-execution of predicted tool calls (based on stable agent control-flow patterns) reduces task completion time by 48.5% and improves throughput by 1.8x.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — AgentForge uses CLI subprocesses not API tool-call loops, making direct application complex; relevant if ProcessRunner ever moves to streaming tool-call interception

summary: March 2026 paper validating a spec-driven autonomous code evolution system over 285+ iterations and 1,094 merged PRs with zero regressions, using synthetic power-user agents and unbeatable test oracles.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — the spec-surface + synthetic-user pattern is compelling for AgentForge's PlanReview stage but would require significant state-machine changes; consider for a future eval-loop issue

summary: April 2026 paper training code LLMs to mentally simulate program execution for self-verification and iterative self-fix without requiring actual execution, using SFT on natural-language execution traces + RLVR.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — the mental-simulation approach could enhance the PlanReview stage prompt but requires model fine-tuning outside AgentForge's scope; monitor for open-source model releases

summary: NVIDIA March 2026 paper decoupling agentic rollout orchestration from RL training via a three-stage async pipeline (INIT/RUN/EVAL), open-sourced as part of NeMo Gym.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — targets RL training infrastructure for model developers, not production orchestration systems; not applicable to AgentForge's CLI-subprocess model

source: Karpathy AutoResearch — Autonomous ML Experiment Loop
summary: Open-source tool that runs ML experiments in a closed loop overnight, modifying code, training for 5 minutes, keeping only improvements; 21k+ stars within days of release in March 2026.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — the closed-loop improvement pattern inspired the episodic memory issue (HID-123); the direct application (running ML experiments) is out of scope for AgentForge

summary: 2026 overview of multi-agent reflection showing that multiple agents reflecting on shared failures from different perspectives outperforms single-agent Reflexion on HotPotQA and HumanEval-Python; avoids local optima of solo reflection.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — the multi-perspective reflection pattern could enhance the AddressingReview loop but would require a second agent pass at remediation stage; table for future exploration

summary: 2026 survey of agent memory architectures covering in-process buffer (working memory), SQLite (episodic), ChromaDB (semantic), and graph-style retrieval; benchmarks common production combinations.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — informs the episodic memory design in HID-123; vector retrieval upgrade is explicitly listed as out-of-scope for that issue and can be revisited here

source: NVIDIA Polar — GRPO Training for Coding Agents
summary: May 2026 NVIDIA post explaining how Polar connects Codex, Claude Code, and Qwen Code to GRPO reinforcement learning for training token-faithful coding agents.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — targets model training, not orchestration; AgentForge consumes trained models as black-box CLI tools and cannot influence model training
